title: Agent as a Tool
constraints: The translator agent must be invoked as a tool by the main agent
The main agent decides when translation is needed
You may not hard-code the target language in the main agent's prompt
The translator must remain a standalone agent, not a plain function
hints: Wrap the translator agent's run method inside a tool function the main agent can call
The main agent's instructions should mention it has access to a translation tool
In OpenAI Agents SDK you can use the agent_tool pattern; in LangChain wrap the agent call in a BaseTool

starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Two separate agents exist but the main agent cannot call the translator
# TODO: Wire the translator agent as a tool available to the main agent

translator = Agent(
    name="Translator",
    instructions="You are a translator. Translate the given text to the requested language.",
)

main_agent = Agent(
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant. You can answer questions and help with tasks.",
)

# The user wants a translated response, but main_agent has no way to call translator
result = Runner.run_sync(main_agent, "Translate 'Hello, how are you?' to French")
print(result.final_output)

result = Runner.run_sync(main_agent, "What is the capital of Japan?")
print(result.final_output)
starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Translator chain exists but is not wired as a tool for the main agent
# TODO: Wrap the translator chain as a tool the main agent can call

translator_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a translator. Translate the given text to the requested language."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
])
translator_chain = translator_prompt | llm

main_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a helpful assistant. You can answer questions and help with tasks."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
    ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"),
])

# Main agent has no tools — it cannot reach the translator
agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, [], main_prompt)
executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=[])

result = executor.invoke({"input": "Translate 'Hello, how are you?' to French"})
print(result["output"])
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Two separate agents exist but the main agent cannot call the translator
# TODO: Wire the translator agent so the main agent can delegate translation tasks

translator = Agent(
    role="Translator",
    goal="Translate text to the requested language",
    backstory="You are a translator. Translate the given text to the requested language.",
    llm=llm,
)

main_agent = Agent(
    role="Assistant",
    goal="Help users with questions and tasks",
    backstory="You are a helpful assistant. You can answer questions and help with tasks.",
    llm=llm,
)

# The user wants a translated response, but only main_agent runs
task = Task(description="Translate 'Hello, how are you?' to French", expected_output="The translated text", agent=main_agent)
crew = Crew(agents=[main_agent], tasks=[task], process=Process.sequential)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Translator wired as a tool
evaluationCriteria.description: The translator agent is callable as a tool from within the main agent

evaluationCriteria.label: Main agent delegates translation
evaluationCriteria.description: The main agent recognizes translation requests and invokes the translator tool

evaluationCriteria.label: Non-translation queries still work
evaluationCriteria.description: Regular questions are answered directly by the main agent without invoking the translator
